key_drivers: Iran war (US-Israel strikes Feb 28) driving geopolitical risk premium: oil spike, gold surge, VIX elevated to 21.44
Real yields flat-to-rising (+3bps to 1.77%) and DXY strengthening (+0.94% to 98.56) creating headwinds for risk assets
BoJ hawkish trajectory (rate at 0.75%, more hikes signaled) supporting JPY structurally; RBA hike to 3.85% bullish AUD fundamentally but risk-off overwhelms
Large crude inventory build (+16MM barrels week ending Feb 20) offsetting geopolitical supply premium for CL
ISM Manufacturing 52.4 and GDPNow 3.0% signal resilient US economy, reducing Fed cut urgency; 96% probability of hold at March 17-18 FOMC

stale_sources: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 (HY OAS last updated Feb 28 - 2 business days stale)
FRED T10Y2Y (2s10s last updated Feb 27 - estimated from 10Y and 2Y yields)
China Caixin Feb 2026 PMI (not yet released, due March 4)
fallbacks_used: TradingEconomics for real-time 2Y yield (3.49%) to estimate 2s10s
Rigzone/TradingEconomics for EIA crude inventory (+16MM barrels)
data_quality.confidence_notes: Geopolitical risk (Iran war) introduces elevated uncertainty across all instruments. HY OAS stale by 2 days; assessed as widening based on VIX spike and market conditions. China Feb PMI not yet available.
